diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Angioedema after starting an ACE inhibitor, without urticaria, strongly indicates bradykinin-mediated ACE-inhibitor angioedema.

symptoms.symptom_name: Deep tense non-urticarial asymmetric swelling; no hives/wheeze/hypotension/GI symptoms

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Deep, tense, non-itchy, asymmetric angioedema without urticaria is the bradykinin pattern.
Absence of urticaria, wheeze, hypotension and GI symptoms argues against anaphylaxis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Airway symptoms (voice change, throat tightness, dysphagia, stridor)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Any airway involvement in angioedema is an immediate life threat mandating emergency airway management.
Direct airway examination is required to confirm patency and cannot be done remotely — hence emergency in-person assessment.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Direct visualisation/monitoring of the airway is the priority; angioedema is a clinical diagnosis.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: C1-esterase inhibitor / complement studies (later, if recurrent or no clear cause)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To evaluate for hereditary/acquired C1-INH deficiency if episodes recur or the ACE-inhibitor link is unclear.
May help distinguish mast-cell (anaphylactic) from bradykinin-mediated angioedema.
